summary: Decompiled five item/Pokemon-related functions across Blastoise/Kamex, Kirby Cutter beam, Lugia, Goomba/Oldkuri, and Pikachu Thunder files, with small header prototype updates for recovered signatures. Evidence includes the PR body, full diff, changed-file metadata, and a decomp-dev bot report; verification evidence is mixed because the PR body claims all five functions matched while the bot report lists three new 100% matches and two near-matches/improvements.

key_files.role: Replaced placeholder for itKamex_UnkMotion1_Anim with C implementation using item timer, velocity, animation update, hitlag callbacks, and accessory callback.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text: lists itKamex_UnkMotion1_Anim and describes Blastoise water gun

key_files.role: Added includes and implemented it_8029BAB8 as a Kirby Cutter beam SpawnItem setup and post-spawn initialization routine.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: itkirbycutterbeam.c hunk adding db/item includes and it_8029BAB8 body
human_pr_text: describes Kirby Cutter beam spawner

key_files.role: Updated it_8029BB90 prototype from UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS to void it_8029BB90(Item_GObj* gobj, HSD_GObj* owner).

Replaced placeholder for it_802D1E8C with SpawnItem construction for Lugia-related item spawning, including previous/current positions, facing direction, velocity from it_802D1F64, parent links, and Item_80268B18 return.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text: describes it_802D1E8C as Lugia flight callback

key_files.role: Updated Lugia prototypes for it_802D1E8C and it_802D1F64 from unknown signatures to typed Item_GObj*/ItemKind/f32 and Vec3-returning forms.

Replaced placeholder for itOldkuri_UnkMotion2_Phys with rolling physics implementation using oldkuri item variables, attributes, velocity updates, floor-normal velocity adjustment, and gravity/physics helper.
Added include for it/it_266F.h and implemented itPikachuthunder_UnkMotion1_Coll with collision branch logic, offset position setup, thunder termination helper, and Pikachu SpecialLw callback checks.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: itpikachuthunder.c include and collision function hunks

what_changed: Five placeholder item functions were replaced with C implementations: itKamex_UnkMotion1_Anim, it_8029BAB8, it_802D1E8C, itOldkuri_UnkMotion2_Phys, and itPikachuthunder_UnkMotion1_Coll.
Two headers were updated to replace unknown prototypes with recovered typed signatures: itkirbycutterbeam.h for it_8029BB90 and itlugia.h for it_802D1E8C/it_802D1F64.
itkirbycutterbeam.c gained db/item includes needed by the new spawner implementation; itpikachuthunder.c gained it/it_266F.h.
The bot report recorded +640 matched code bytes, three new 100% matches, and two additional function improvements that did not appear as 100% in the bot output.
smart_moves: Recovered header prototypes alongside callsite/body usage instead of leaving UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS for functions whose signatures were needed by the new code.
Used existing item subsystem structures and helpers such as SpawnItem, Item_80268B18, GET_ITEM, Item_GObj, HSD_GObj, ItemKind, and item-var union arms instead of raw pointer-only code.
Kept unknown address-style names such as it_802D1E8C and it_8029BAB8 where the diff did not prove semantic global names.
Captured item spawner behavior through field-by-field SpawnItem initialization, including parent gobj links, facing direction, damage, velocity, and flags.

decomp_lessons.lesson: When a recovered item function proves related callee signatures, update the owning header prototypes in the same PR rather than preserving UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: itkirbycutterbeam.h changes it_8029BB90 to void it_8029BB90(Item_GObj* gobj, HSD_GObj* owner)
raw_diff: itlugia.h changes it_802D1E8C and it_802D1F64 signatures

decomp_lessons.lesson: Item-spawn decompilation can often be expressed with the existing SpawnItem struct and Item_80268B18 helper, with parent links and flags made explicit.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: itkirbycutterbeam.c it_8029BAB8 initializes SpawnItem and calls Item_80268B18
raw_diff: itlugia.c it_802D1E8C initializes SpawnItem and returns Item_80268B18

decomp_lessons.lesson: Use the correct item-var union arms and typed attribute structs when they are available, even when fields remain partially named by offsets.
Treat author-stated verification and bot reports as separate evidence sources; do not collapse them when they disagree on exact match status.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text: claims all 5 functions 100% match verified by overnight script
text_corpus: decomp-dev bot reports 3 new matches and 3 improvements, with itPikachuthunder_UnkMotion1_Coll at 99.80% and itKamex_UnkMotion1_Anim at 99.44%

naming_conventions.convention: Preserve address-style global names when the PR does not prove a semantic name.
Use established short local names for obvious item/spawn roles.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: local variables in itkamex.c, itkirbycutterbeam.c, itlugia.c, itoldkuri.c, itpikachuthunder.c

naming_conventions.convention: Keep existing function family prefixes for item behavior callbacks.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: placeholder comments replaced by same-named function bodies

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Narrow PAD_STACK(8) use was retained in three implemented functions.
assembly_or_matching_tactics.notes: The PR body claims all five functions matched by overnight script, but the bot report lists Kamex and Pikachu Thunder as sub-100% improvements; keep PAD_STACK as a tactic needing curator/reviewer caution rather than a general style lesson.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Field initialization order in SpawnItem was written explicitly, likely to match generated code shape for item spawning.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: SpawnItem field assignments in itkirbycutterbeam.c
raw_diff: SpawnItem field assignments in itlugia.c

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Some functions re-fetch GET_ITEM into a shadowed ip inside a nested block, suggesting local lifetime/register-shape steering.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: nested Item* ip = GET_ITEM(gobj) in itkamex.c
raw_diff: nested Item* ip = GET_ITEM(gobj) in itoldkuri.c

review_feedback.feedback: Reported matched code 59.38% (+0.02%, +640 bytes), with 3 new matches: it_8029BAB8, it_802D1E8C, and itOldkuri_UnkMotion2_Phys at 100%.
Reported improvements, not full 100% matches, for itPikachuthunder_UnkMotion1_Coll at 99.80%, itKamex_UnkMotion1_Anim at 99.44%, and itkirbycutterbeam .sdata2 at 12.50%.
No review comments or reviews were included in the slice.

follow_up_queries: Find later PRs touching itKamex_UnkMotion1_Anim or itPikachuthunder_UnkMotion1_Coll to see whether the 99.44%/99.80% bot-reported improvements were later resolved.
Search for PAD_STACK(8) in item/Pokemon functions to identify whether this PR's usage became cleaned up or remained as a matching tactic.
Search for SpawnItem initialization patterns in item spawner functions using Item_80268B18, especially parent gobj and x44_flag.b0 setup.
Search for header updates replacing UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS in src/melee/it/items headers around recovered item functions.
Search for it_8029BB90 signature usage after this PR to confirm consistency with Item_GObj* and HSD_GObj* owner parameters.

evidence_refs: metadata_json: PR #2342 metadata and changed files
human_pr_text: PR body summary and claimed verification
raw_diff: full diff for seven changed files
notes: The raw diff and changed-file metadata strongly support what files changed and what code was added.

The PR body claims all five functions are 100% matched by an overnight script, but the loaded bot report says only three functions are new 100% matches and lists Kamex/Pikachu Thunder as near-match improvements.
No human review comments are present in the loaded slice, so reviewer intent and requested changes cannot be inferred.
The PR body contains generated-by-Claude text; semantic descriptions are useful PR evidence but should not outrank code/diff evidence.
No independent build, objdiff, or source lookup was performed during intake.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Header prototypes should be updated when recovered item bodies prove signatures
accepted_candidate_records.text: PR #2342 replaced UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS prototypes in item headers after implementing functions that used typed callees/returns, aligning with truthful header practice.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: itlugia.h prototypes for it_802D1E8C and it_802D1F64

accepted_candidate_records.title: PAD_STACK and shadowed locals appeared as narrow matching tactics in item functions
accepted_candidate_records.text: PR #2342 used PAD_STACK(8) and nested GET_ITEM re-fetches in a few item functions. Because verification evidence is mixed for two functions, this should be remembered as a narrow tactic needing objdiff evidence, not as general source style.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: PAD_STACK(8) in Kamex, Oldkuri, and Pikachu Thunder functions
raw_diff: nested GET_ITEM re-fetches in Kamex and Oldkuri

accepted_candidate_records.title: SpawnItem-based item spawners can expose typed structure without semantic renaming
accepted_candidate_records.text: PR #2342 implemented address-named item spawners with SpawnItem field setup and Item_80268B18 while preserving address-style global names where semantics were not proven.

rejection_notes: Do not promote the PR body's 'all 5 functions 100% match' claim as an accepted validation result without reconciling it with the bot report showing only three new 100% matches and two near-matches.
Do not treat PAD_STACK(8), shadowed ip locals, or exact field assignment order as preferred style; the evidence supports them only as narrow matching tactics in this PR.
Do not invent semantic names for it_8029BAB8, it_802D1E8C, or related address-style symbols based only on the PR body's functional descriptions.
